self_report.reflection: Setting area(A) = area(B) collapses to ∫_0^{x_2}(c - f(x))dx = 0, giving c = x_2 - 3x_2^3/4. Combined with c = 2x_2 - 3x_2^3 (point on curve), this yields x_2 = 2/3 and c = 4/9. Verified that 2(2/3) - 3(2/3)^3 = 4/3 - 8/9 = 4/9.
self_report.notes: Standard Putnam 1993 A-1 derivation produces c = 4/9 exactly as claimed.
